status: Stage S3 (L2/L3/L4 Build) COMPLETE 5/5 — S3.T5 eaasp-cli-v2 COMPLETE @ a638bc5. Plan 12/15 (S3 5/5, S4 pending 3 tasks). NEW Python typer CLI at tools/eaasp-cli-v2/ — 10 source + 9 test files, 1592 lines. Four sub-apps (session/memory/skill/policy) = 14 commands. Endpoints all env-configurable via EAASP_{SKILL,L2,L3,L4}_URL, defaults to 18081/18083/18084/18085 (inherits S3.T4.5 port remap @ 85c5c6e). CliConfig dataclass + from_env. ServiceClient wraps httpx.AsyncClient with uniform error projection (CliError exit codes 2=4xx/3=connect/4=5xx/1=other). _client_factory injection hook monkeypatched by tests — MockTransport wraps injected AsyncClient, no runtime respx dep. Rich-based output (print_table/print_json/print_panel/print_error). session show walks L4 GET /v1/sessions/{id} + GET /v1/sessions/{id}/events and renders Evidence pack (anchor_id extraction from event payloads). skill run = sugar: session create + send 'run skill'. session list is D41 stub (Rich panel + exit 0). Tests 19/19 via typer.testing.CliRunner + httpx.MockTransport: 2 config (defaults + env override) + 3 client (happy/404/ConnectError) + 4 session (create-happy/create-503/show/send) + 3 memory (search/read/list) + 3 skill (list/submit/promote) + 3 policy (deploy-happy/deploy-422/mode) + 1 D41 notice. RuFlo swarm-1775934399764 trace: scout blueprint (10 src + 9 test files matrix, 14-command endpoint table cross-referenced against routes.rs/models.rs/api.py/mcp_tools.py, test plan) → coder 19/19 first-pass with 5 blueprint deviations all verified against real service code: (1) skill-registry bodies are FLAT per routes.rs Json<T> (NOT wrapped in {args} like L2), (2) skill submit uses {id,name,description,version,frontmatter_yaml,prose} matching SubmitDraftRequest in models.rs, (3) skill promote adds <version> positional per InvokePromote, (4) Click 8.3 removed mix_stderr=False kwarg, (5) list commands render list-root responses permissively. reviewer APPROVE-WITH-COMMENTS no Criticals — 14 commands cross-validated against actual routes.rs/models.rs/api.py/mcp_tools.py line-by-line, port audit clean (no 808x), tests reviewed. 4 new Deferred D42-D45 appended to plan. ═══ PRIOR S3.T4.5 @ 85c5c6e. Cross-cutting port remap per user requirement that 808x are too common and ports must never be hardcoded. All default ports moved to ≥10000 with last two digits preserved: skill-registry 8081→18081, mcp-orchestrator 8082→18082, l3-governance 8083→18083, l4-orchestration 8084→18084, l2-memory-engine 8085→18085. Rust crates (skill-registry, mcp-orchestrator) gained clap `env` feature — --port/--host now read from EAASP_{SKILL_REGISTRY,MCP_ORCHESTRATOR}_{PORT,HOST}. Hardcoded `0.0.0.0:{port}` bind strings replaced with `{host}:{port}`. L4 handshake defaults (L2_URL_DEFAULT, L3_URL_DEFAULT) and L4 test fixtures (test_api.py, test_intents.py L2_DEFAULT/L3_DEFAULT) moved to 1808x so respx mocks continue to match. L2 memory-engine ENV var renamed EAASP_MEMORY_PORT → EAASP_L2_PORT (legacy alias kept one release cycle). Docs updated: EAASP_v2_0_MVP_SCOPE.md §3.2-3.3 port table + plan verification curl examples + S3.T5 config path line. Archived v1.8 docs intentionally left alone (historical snapshot). Tests post-remap all green: L2 47/47 · L3 28/28 · L4 31/31 · Rust skill-registry+mcp-orchestrator 14 tests. ═══ PRIOR S3.T4 eaasp-l4-orchestration COMPLETE. New Python FastAPI service at tools/eaasp-l4-orchestration/ (port 8084) implementing L4 orchestration plane: Contract 2 Intent Gateway (POST /v1/intents/dispatch), Contract 5 Session Control (POST /v1/sessions/create three-way handshake + POST /v1/sessions/{id}/message + GET /v1/sessions/{id}/events?from&to&limit + GET /v1/sessions/{id}). SQLite schema: sessions (state machine: created|active|closed|failed, payload_json snapshot) + session_events (append-only AUTOINCREMENT seq, FK to sessions). Three-way handshake sequence (session_orchestrator.create_session): (1) L2 POST /api/v1/memory/search → P3 MemoryRefs, (2) L3 POST /v1/sessions/{id}/validate → P1 PolicyContext.hooks, (3) context_assembly.build_session_payload assembles P1-P5 dict + budget flags, (4) single BEGIN IMMEDIATE transaction inserts sessions row + SESSION_CREATED event + RUNTIME_INITIALIZE_STUBBED event (D27 L1 gRPC stub), (5) returns session handle. Tests 31/31 (5 event_stream + 7 handshake + 7 session_orchestrator + 10 api + 2 intents): `cd tools/eaasp-l4-orchestration && uv run pytest`. RuFlo swarm-1775926706453 trace: scout blueprint (600 words, file layout + schema + handshake pseudocode + Deferred D27-D33 map) → coder single-pass 31/31 first run → reviewer APPROVE-WITH-COMMENTS (no Criticals, 4 Major M1-M4, 7 Minor N1-N7, inherited D18/D22/D23/D24 resurfaced as D27-D36) → inline fixes applied: M4 PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000 in db.py::connect, N1 to_seq<from_seq raises ValueError, N3 SESSION_CREATED seq<RUNTIME_INITIALIZE_STUBBED seq assertion → 31/31 final. Design decisions: (1) create_session uses ONE connection + ONE BEGIN IMMEDIATE for 3 INSERTs (avoids two-connection WAL deadlock), (2) UpstreamError(service, kind, detail) → L2/L3 ConnectError→503 unavailable, L3 404→424 no_policy, 5xx→502 error, (3) httpx.AsyncClient injected via FastAPI lifespan + test fixture override for respx interception, (4) _sanitize_errors copied verbatim from S3.T3, (5) port 8084 via EAASP_L4_PORT env. Inherits S3.T2/T3 C1 (BEGIN IMMEDIATE), C3 (limit ≤500), M1 (_sanitize_errors), WAL+foreign_keys+busy_timeout. Out of scope: real L1 gRPC Initialize/Send (stubbed as events), NLU intent→skill resolution (caller passes skill_id), WebSocket streaming, cursor pagination. 14 new Deferred (D27-D40): L1 gRPC / global exception handler / session_id path regex / busy_timeout L2-L3 backport / structured logging / concurrency stress / payload double-encoding / NLU / WebSocket / cursor pagination / trim flag wiring / user_id RBAC passthrough / policy_version hash / sessions state machine. S3.T3 status verbatim preserved below. ═══ PRIOR S3.T3 @ 0907d13. New Python FastAPI service at tools/eaasp-l3-governance/ (port 8083) implementing thin L3 governance: Contract 1 Policy Deployment (PUT /v1/policies/managed-hooks + GET /v1/policies/versions + PUT /v1/policies/{hook_id}/mode), Contract 4 Telemetry Ingest (POST+GET /v1/telemetry/events), Contract 5 partial Session validate (POST /v1/sessions/{id}/validate handshake stub). SQLite schema: managed_settings_versions (append-only versioned JSON blobs), managed_hooks_mode_overrides (upsert-in-place, floats above versions), telemetry_events (session-indexed PostToolUse payloads). Tests 28/28 (9 policy_engine + 6 audit + 3 session_validate + 10 api): `cd tools/eaasp-l3-governance && uv run pytest`. RuFlo swarm-1775925175221 trace: coder single-pass 26/26 first run → reviewer APPROVE-WITH-COMMENTS (no Criticals, 3 Major M1-M3 deferred, 4 Minor) → R1+R2 tests applied inline (sanitize serializer round-trip + 10x concurrent deploy contiguous versions) → 28/28 final. Design decisions: (1) mode overrides as separate table not version bump so history stays meaningful, (2) _sanitize_errors() projects Pydantic v2 ValidationError.ctx to JSON-safe dict preventing 500s, (3) ManagedSettings ConfigDict(extra='allow') for forward-compat, (4) received_at as TEXT for lexicographic since-query ordering, (5) hook_matches() strict wildcard semantics (None/'*' matches all, missing field on constrained hook does NOT match). Inherits S3.T2 C1 (BEGIN IMMEDIATE), C3 (limit clamp ≤500), M4 (mode state transition), N4 (404/422 status map), WAL+foreign_keys. Out of scope (Phase 3): approval gates, OPA, evidence chain, policy DSL compile, cross-tenant inheritance, PRE_POLICY_DEPLOY emission. 19 new Deferred (D1-D19): MCP facade / mode history / DSL compile / approval / OPA / evidence chain / rate limit / error taxonomy / deny-precedence resolver / integration test / lifecycle event / contract test / SQLite RETURNING / _sanitize_errors backport / version retention / telemetry TTL / global exception handler / structured logging / pyrightconfig. Plan progress 10/15. Next: S3.T4 eaasp-l4-orchestration (Session Orchestrator + 3-way handshake initiator + Intent Gateway + Event Stream placeholder, port 8084).
